title: A backing pad arrangement for an abrading system, and the abrading system
abstract: The application relates to a backing pad arrangement suitable for an abrading system. The backing pad arrangement comprises a backing pad (413, 513, 523), and an additional layer (418, 518, 528) or an abrading article (417, 527) adapted to be attached to the backing pad (413, 513, 523) with at least one fixing member (319, 419, 519, 529), which at least one fixing member (319, 419, 519, 529) is hollow in its longitudinal direction. The application further relates to the abrading system comprising the backing pad arrangement according to the application.
claims:
Claims Claims: 1 . A backing pad arrangement for an abrading system, the backing pad arrangement comprising a backing pad (413, 513, 523), wherein a lower surface of the backing pad (413, 513, 523) comprises at least one opening, which at least one opening is adapted to receive a fixing member (319, 419, 519, 529), and the backing pad arrangement further comprises: - an additional layer (418, 518, 528) adapted to be attached to the backing pad (413, 513, 523), which additional layer (418, 518, 528) comprises an upper surface, a lower surface and at least one opening (416, 516, 526) which extends through the additional layer (418, 518, 528), and the additional layer is attached to the backing pad (413, 513, 523) with at least one fixing member (319, 419, 519, 529), or - an abrading article (417, 527) adapted to be attached to the backing pad (413, 513, 523), which abrading article comprises at least one opening, which extends through the abrading article (417, 527), and the abrading article (417, 527) is attached to the backing pad (413, 513, 523) with at least one fixing member (319, 419, 519, 529), wherein the at least one fixing member (319, 419, 519, 529) is hollow in its longitudinal direction in order to transport debris with air through the at least one fixing member (319, 419, 519, 529). 2. The backing pad arrangement according to claim 1 , wherein the additional layer (418, 518, 528) or the abrading article (417, 527) is attached to the backing pad (413, 513, 523) with two or more fixing members (319, 419, 519, 529). 3. The backing pad arrangement according to claim 1 , wherein the additional layer (418, 518, 528) or the abrading article (417, 527) is attached to the backing pad (413, 513, 523) with one fixing member (319, 419, 519, 529) arranged in the center of the backing pad (413, 513, 523). 4. The backing pad arrangement according to any of claims 1 -3, wherein the fixing member (319, 419, 519, 529) is of screw-, snap hook- or expanding type. 5. The backing pad arrangement according to any of claims 1 -4, wherein the backing pad (413, 513, 523) comprises at least one air inlet conduit (534) for conducting air to an abrading surface. 6. The backing pad arrangement according to any of claims 1 -5, wherein the additional layer (418, 518, 528) comprises a soft intermediate layer between the upper surface and the lower surface of the additional layer (418, 518, 528). 7. The backing pad arrangement according to any of claims 1 -6, wherein the lower surface of the additional layer (418, 518, 528) comprises abrasive particles. 8. The backing pad arrangement according to any of claims 1 -7, wherein the backing pad (413, 513, 523) comprises a suction conduit formed by the at least one opening (416, 516, 526) of the additional layer (418, 518, 528) or the abrading article (417, 527) and an additional opening (422, 532) of a cover plate (41 1 , 521 ) of the backing pad (413, 513, 523). 9. An abrading system comprising an abrading apparatus, wherein the abrading system further comprises a backing pad arrangement according to any of claims 1 -8. 10. The abrading system according to claim 9, wherein the abrading system comprises a debris extractor system adapted to provide suction pressure and to remove debris formed during abrading work. 1 1 . The abrading system according to claim 10, wherein the debris extractor system is arranged to guide debris away from the additional layer (418, 518, 528) and the abrading surface. 12. The abrading system according to claim 10 or 1 1 , wherein the debris extractor system is adapted to guide debris via at least one of the following: - the additional layer (418, 518, 528), - the abrading article (417, 527), - the backing pad (413, 513, 523), 

 - the fixing member(s) (319, 419, 519, 529), - opening(s) of the additional layer (418, 518, 528), - opening(s) of the abrading article (417, 527), - opening(s) (412, 522, 422, 532) of a cover plate (41 1 , 521 ) of the backing pad (413, 513, 523). 13. The abrading system according to any of claims 9-12, wherein - opening(s) (416, 516, 526) of the additional layer (418, 518, 528) or opening(s) of the abrading article (417, 527), and - opening(s) (412, 522, 422, 532) of the cover plate (41 1 , 521 ) of the backing pad (413, 513, 523) are connected in order to form conduit(s) for removing debris.
